examples.label: 3-vendor SaaS RFP — clear winner with mitigation

input.user_message: We ran an RFP for an enterprise observability platform. Here are the three responses. Score them and tell me who to pick.
input.objective: Replace incumbent observability stack across 4 business units; 3-year horizon; ~12,000 hosts at peak.

vendors.name: Supplier A
vendors.notes: Robust API, mature integrations, public roadmap aligned

vendors.name: Supplier B
vendors.notes: Lower headline price but heavier integration lift; SOC 2 not yet issued

vendors.name: Supplier C
vendors.notes: Premium incumbent in space; highest cost; deepest capability

attribution.cta: Invite Supplier A to BAFO in Aavenir RFPflow

decision_frame.scope: 12,000 hosts at peak, 4 business units, 3-year horizon
decision_frame.business_outcome: Reduce MTTR and consolidate from incumbent stack

assumptions: 0% annual escalator (vendor did not state — flagged)

cost.rationale: USD 265k 3-year TCO; ~5% above lowest. Implementation fee fair.

capability.rationale: Robust API, mature integrations, roadmap aligned with our consolidation goal.

risk.rationale: Stable vendor; 8 reference customers similar to our scale.

compliance.rationale: SOC 2 Type II current, ISO 27001, standard GDPR DPA.

strategic_fit.rationale: Roadmap aligns with our cloud + on-prem hybrid posture.

red_flags.description: Annual escalator not stated — exposes us to first-renewal surprise.
red_flags.evidence: No escalator clause in submitted pricing exhibit.

capability.rationale: Functional but only 3 reference customers; Pro tier capability gaps surfaced in technical scoring.

risk.rationale: SOC 2 Type II in progress (not yet issued); shorter customer list; weaker audit rights in DPA.

compliance.rationale: No ISO 27001, SOC 2 not issued, custom DPA with weak audit rights.

delivery.rationale: 14-week implementation vs 6 weeks for Supplier A — material business cost.

strategic_fit.rationale: Capable but no public roadmap commitment in our key area.

red_flags.description: SOC 2 Type II not yet issued — vendor cannot meet our minimum infosec floor.
red_flags.evidence: RFP response Section 8: 'Type II in progress, expected Q4'.

red_flags.description: 14-week implementation more than 2x Supplier A — delay cost to consolidation program.

vendors.name: Supplier C

assumptions: Enterprise Plus tier includes premium support

capability.rationale: Deepest feature set; 99.99% uptime SLA; HIPAA BAA included.

risk.rationale: Market leader; 14 reference customers; financially stable.

strategic_fit.rationale: Strong roadmap but heavier than our hybrid posture needs.

gaps: Highest cost — premium not justified vs Supplier A for our use case

primary.rationale: Best weighted score (4.15). Balanced cost/capability/compliance fit. Implementation timeline aligned with consolidation deadline.
conditions: Negotiate explicit annual escalator cap (e.g., CPI or 5% max)
Confirm support response times in MSA

fallback.rationale: If budget expands or HIPAA scope is added, Supplier C becomes justifiable at premium. Score 3.80, no red flags.

not_recommended.reason: Score 3.05 below 'qualified yes' threshold. Two high/medium red flags: SOC 2 not issued (infosec floor) and 14-week implementation (delivery risk).

executive_summary: Recommend Supplier A — best weighted score (4.15) and best cost-capability-compliance balance.
Decisive factors: full SOC 2 + ISO, roadmap fit, 6-week implementation, ~$80k delta vs the premium incumbent.
Top trade-off: capability ceiling lower than Supplier C; acceptable for our hybrid scope.
Condition: negotiate annual escalator cap before signature (currently unstated).
Next step: invite Supplier A to BAFO with two negotiation asks (escalator cap, support SLAs); hold Supplier C as fallback.
